$doc: BMD-derived input→output pairs for Newton-Raphson IRR (monthly, annualized ×12). Used by both ehc_contracts.metrics.irr and the Reporting App's R twin at ehc-board-reporting-app/Support/irr.R and the Board Auditor's independent replica in ehc-data-analysis/data_board_auditor/tests/test_auditor_bmd_parity.py. Every case specifies the cashflow sequence and the expected annualized IRR, plus per-implementation caller params when relevant. Tolerance for numeric parity is ±0.001 percentage points = 1e-5 absolute on the decimal-form IRR, tighter than the BMD's ±0.01% display tolerance per Phase 4.3 ratification 2026-04-22. Edge cases assert behavior under each on_nonconvergence mode: 'best_estimate' (Dashboard/ProjFin), 'none' (Auditor), 'raise' (R twin via safe_irr_custom tryCatch). Expected values for closed-form cases derived analytically: NPV(r) = 0 solved for monthly r, then ×12.
tolerance_description: Absolute tolerance in decimal form on the annualized IRR. Corresponds to ±0.001 percentage points on the rendered percent (e.g., 9.420% vs 9.421% within tolerance). Per Phase 4.3 ratification 2026-04-22.

cases.name: break_even_1_month
cases.description: Simplest closed-form case: -100 at t=0, +100 at t=1. NPV(r) = -100 + 100/(1+r) = 0 → r = 0 → annualized = 0.

cases.name: break_even_2_months
cases.description: Same break-even over 2 periods: -100 at t=0, 0 at t=1, +100 at t=2. NPV(r) = -100 + 100/(1+r)^2 = 0 → r = 0.

cases.name: one_pct_monthly
cases.description: 1% monthly return = 12% annualized. -100 at t=0, +101 at t=1 solves (1+r)=1.01 → r=0.01 → annualized = 0.12.

cases.name: ten_pct_monthly
cases.description: 10% monthly return = 120% annualized. -100 at t=0, +110 at t=1 solves (1+r)=1.10 → r=0.10 → annualized = 1.20.

cases.name: two_period_compound_10pct
cases.description: 10% monthly compounded over 2 periods. -100 at t=0, 0 at t=1, +121 at t=2 solves (1+r)^2 = 1.21 → r = 0.10 → annualized = 1.20.

cases.name: twenty_month_compound
cases.description: -1000 at t=0, 0 for 18 periods, +1200 at t=19. (1+r)^19 = 1.20 → r = 1.20^(1/19) - 1 = 0.009642059404… monthly → annualized = 0.115704712848… 19th-period bullet repayment shape.

cases.name: land_bank_shape
cases.description: Realistic land-banking cashflow: three periods of investment (-500, -200, -100) followed by six periods of increasing returns (50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500). Total invested 800, total returned 1550, net 750 over 9 periods. Expected annualized IRR reference-computed at tight convergence.

cases.name: small_loss
cases.description: Cashflow that produces a small negative IRR. -100 at t=0, +99 at t=1 solves (1+r) = 0.99 → r = -0.01 → annualized = -0.12.

cases.name: breakeven_with_zeros
cases.description: -100 at t=0, seven months of zero, +100 at t=8. Same break-even shape as the 2-period case, exercising more iterations of the discount series. NPV(r) = -100 + 100/(1+r)^8 = 0 → r = 0 → annualized = 0.

edge_cases.name: empty_list
edge_cases.description: Empty cashflows. Always returns None regardless of on_nonconvergence — the len<2 guard fires before iteration.

edge_cases.name: single_cashflow
edge_cases.description: Length-1 cashflow. Always returns None — IRR is undefined on a single point.

edge_cases.name: all_zeros_three_months
edge_cases.description: All-zero cashflow triggers the dnpv_eps path on the first iteration. 'best_estimate' returns guess × 12 = 0.01 × 12 = 0.12 (current Dashboard/ProjFin behavior, preserved for back-compat — in practice safe_irr_custom pre-filters this in R and the emitters' callers rarely see it). 'none' returns None. 'raise' raises IRRNonConvergenceError.

param_parity_cases.name: land_bank_python_vs_r_params
param_parity_cases.description: Same cashflow, run with Python defaults (max_iter=1000, tol=1e-8) vs R twin defaults (max_iter=100, tol=1e-6). Both must agree within the ±1e-5 fixture tolerance — proves that the parameter difference between the emitters and the R twin is invisible at display precision.

param_parity_cases.name: twenty_month_python_vs_r_params
param_parity_cases.description: Long cashflow (20 periods) with single terminal payout. Tests that R's looser tolerance still converges to the same display value.
